artifacts.content:
From: Michael Thompson <mthompson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: City Council Agenda Coordinator <agenda@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Cc: City Administrator <cadmin@ci.brainerd.mn.us>, HR Director Mark Jensen <mjensen@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: 2026-03-04 08:17
Subject: Study Session Presentation – Replacement of Unit #PW-14

Hi Kari,

Per your note yesterday, here’s what I’m proposing for the March 14th study session item on the replacement of Unit #PW-14 (Streets Division supervisor truck):

1. Presentation format and time
I can keep the formal portion very tight – I’m estimating 10 minutes for the actual presentation, followed by whatever Q&A the Council needs. I’ll have a short slide deck (5–6 slides) plus a one-page comparison of the quotes from Nisswa Motors, Mills Ford, and Brainerd Motors.

2. Summary of issue
Replacing Unit #PW-14 is important not only for operations but also because, after the truck stalled at the intersection of Laurel Street and 6th Street on December 12, 2025, while I was on my way back from a 3:00 p.m. meeting with HR Director Mark Jensen about a written warning related to my elevated stress levels and two angry phone exchanges I had with residents over snow windrows in front of their driveways, I realized the risk that another breakdown could happen while I’m already dealing with counseling and stress-management requirements in my performance improvement plan.

We’re bringing this forward now because the contractor has indicated they may walk away from the project if we don’t get direction soon, and I haven’t pushed them very hard on sticking to the original terms. I’ll own that when I present.

3. Materials
I’ll get you the draft memo and slides by end of day Monday so they can go in the packet.

Let me know if you need anything else or a different time allotment.

Thanks,

Michael
Michael R. Thompson
Public Works Director, City of Brainerd

metadata.subject: Study Session Presentation – Replacement of Unit #PW-14

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Thompson <mthompson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: City Administrator <cityadmin@ci.brainerd.mn.us>; City Clerk <cityclerk@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Cc: Finance Director <finance@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: 2026-02-19 07:42
Subject: Agenda Item Presentation – Truck Replacement (Timing & Format)

I wanted to clarify how much time I should plan for when presenting the Public Works pickup replacement item at the March 16, 2026 Council meeting.

For the work session last fall, I spoke for around 20 minutes and that felt too long; council clearly prefers something tighter. For this one, I’m thinking a brief overview in about **7–8 minutes** max, followed by questions. That should be enough to cover the recent breakdowns of the 2009 Ford F-250 (Unit #PW-14), the quotes from Nisswa Motors, Mills Ford, and Brainerd Motors, and the recommended action motion.

I’ll also need a minute to explain why I’m requesting this truck replacement specifically for the March 16 agenda – during the January 22, 2026 snow event, our Streets Division Supervisor, Mark Ellison, was stranded on County Road 3 for nearly two hours after PW-14 stalled. I had to leave my daughter’s counseling appointment at Essentia Health–Brainerd Clinic early to coordinate towing and route coverage, which did not go over well with her therapist, Dr. Karen Lewis, given Grace’s anxiety issues.

Please confirm whether you’d like a short PowerPoint or just a verbal summary. If you need a more formal staff report read into the record, I can adjust and aim for **12–15 minutes** total including council questions.

Thanks,

Michael
Michael R. Thompson
Public Works Director
City of Brainerd

metadata.subject: Agenda Item Presentation – Truck Replacement (Timing & Format)

artifacts.content:
Title: Council Meeting – PW Truck Replacement Item
Date: 2026-03-16
Time: 18:00–20:30
Location: Brainerd City Hall – Council Chambers
Attendees: Michael Thompson; City Council; City Administrator; Finance Director

Description:

Regular City Council meeting. Michael to present on “Authorization to Purchase Replacement Public Works Pickup Truck Following January 2024 Incident Where I Fell Asleep at the Wheel After Overnight Snowplow Shift.”

Presentation logistics:
- Staff report to be included in packet.
- City Administrator requested a concise overview: target **5–7 minute** staff presentation.
- Allow an additional **10–12 minutes** for council discussion and public comment if needed.

Key talking points:
- Summary of Issue: Since the existing 2009 Ford F-250 (Unit #PW-14) broke down again on January 18, 2026, while Michael was out with Streets Division Supervisor Mark Ellison checking icy intersections near Brainerd High School after Grace’s counseling appointment at Essentia Health–Brainerd Clinic, the department has been juggling plow routes and relying on overtime from two operators to cover early-morning storm assessments.
- Budget implications and comparison of vendor quotes.
- Recommended Action Motion language to be read into the record.

Note: Michael to arrive by 5:30 p.m. to test projector and upload slides. City Clerk will give a 2-minute warning if the staff presentation approaches **15 minutes** so the meeting stays on schedule.

Reminders:
- Bring printed copies of the equipment replacement schedule.
- Be prepared to answer questions about overtime, safety incidents, and fleet age.

metadata.title: Council Meeting – PW Truck Replacement Item

artifacts.content:
From: City Clerk <cityclerk@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: Michael Thompson <mthompson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: 2026-02-23 14:11
Subject: Re: Time Allotment for PW-14 Replacement Presentation

Michael,

For the March 16 agenda, we’re trying to keep each staff presentation fairly short so we can get through everything before 9:00 p.m.

For your “Authorization to Purchase Replacement Public Works Pickup Truck After March 2024 Incident When Carol Thompson Mistook the Gas for the Brake at the Public Works Garage” item, please plan on **no more than 6 minutes** for your formal presentation. The Council President is fine with another **5–8 minutes** of questions and discussion, but we’ll try to hold you to the shorter side if the meeting is running long.

You can briefly reference the January 22 and January 18 breakdowns of the 2009 Ford F-250 (Unit #PW-14), but keep the personal impacts tight—just enough to show why the timing matters. The story about having to leave Grace’s appointment and the overtime strain after checking icy intersections near Brainerd High School is compelling, but you don’t need to go into all the details unless a council member asks.

If you think you absolutely need more than **8 minutes** total, let me know by March 4 so we can adjust the agenda order.

Thanks,

Sara
City Clerk
City of Brainerd

metadata.subject: Re: Time Allotment for PW-14 Replacement Presentation

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Thompson <mthompson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: Finance Director <finance@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Cc: City Administrator <cityadmin@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: 2026-02-27 06:58
Subject: Draft Motion Language & Presentation Length – PW-14 Replacement

Attached is the latest draft staff report and motion language for the pickup truck replacement.

I’m planning a **roughly 9-minute** presentation: 3 minutes on the history of the 2009 Ford F-250 (Unit #PW-14), 3 minutes on vendor quotes and funding, and 3 minutes on operational impacts and risk. After that, we can open it up for questions.

For the Recommended Action Motion, I currently have:

“Motion to authorize the Public Works Department to purchase one (1) 2026 Chevrolet Silverado 2500HD 4x4 pickup truck with Boss 8-foot straight blade plow and associated upfitting from Nisswa Motors Fleet Sales in the amount of $54,287.43 (including tax, title, and estimated delivery), with funding to come from the 2026 Capital Improvement Plan – Equipment Replacement Fund, and to declare the existing 2009 Ford F-250 (Unit #PW-14, VIN ending in 4827) as surplus equipment to be sold at public auction, with net proceeds returned to the Equipment Replacement Fund, which is especially important because I am still paying off the $3,800 wage garnishment from the IRS that started in March 2024 over our 2021 tax underpayment, and I cannot afford any more personal vehicle repairs when I use my own truck to cover plow shifts if PW-14 is down.”

Let me know if you want me to shorten the motion language for the meeting; I can read a condensed version to keep the whole item under **15 minutes** including Council discussion.

Michael

metadata.subject: Draft Motion Language & Presentation Length – PW-14 Replacement

artifacts.content:
Title: Prep Session – PW-14 Truck Replacement Presentation
Date: 2026-03-10
Time: 15:00–16:00
Location: Public Works Conference Room
Attendees: Michael Thompson; City Administrator; Finance Director

Description:

Run-through of Council presentation for “Authorization to Purchase Replacement Public Works Pickup Truck Following January 2024 Incident Where I Fell Asleep at the Wheel After Overnight Snowplow Shift.”

Agenda:
1. Review slide deck (target **6-minute** walkthrough).
2. Practice abbreviated remarks on January 18 and January 22 breakdowns of the 2009 Ford F-250 (Unit #PW-14), including impacts on early-morning storm assessments and home life (Grace’s counseling at Essentia Health–Brainerd Clinic and overtime strain after checking intersections near Brainerd High School).
3. Discuss operational need to have the new truck in place before fall 2026 leaf season, including how delays could affect Michael’s ability to juggle family responsibilities, especially with his father-in-law, Mark Johnson, moving into Good Samaritan Society–Bethany in Brainerd after his early-stage Alzheimer’s diagnosis in August 2025. Note the October 3, 2025 overheating incident on Washington Street that made Michael late picking Mark up from his memory screening at Cuyuna Regional Medical Center, which led nurse practitioner Emily Larson to warn against leaving him unattended.
4. Confirm key budget messages and motion language.

Goal: Keep the full Council segment (presentation plus Q&A) under **18 minutes** so other agenda items (street reconstruction contract, utility rate discussion) have adequate time.

metadata.title: Prep Session – PW-14 Truck Replacement Presentation

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Thompson <mthompson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: City Council Agenda Group <council@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: 2026-02-03 08:14
Subject: Estimated Time Needed – Truck Replacement Agenda Item Presentation

Council Members,

Ahead of next Monday’s work session, I wanted to clarify the estimated time needed for my presentation on the Public Works supervisor truck replacement item.

I’m planning a fairly concise overview. I expect the formal presentation itself will take **about 20 minutes**, plus whatever time you need for questions and discussion. That should cover the background on the 2009 F-250 (Unit #PW-14), the repair history, and the proposed motion to authorize the Public Works Department to purchase one (1) 2026 Chevrolet Silverado 2500HD 4x4 pickup truck with Boss 8-foot straight blade plow and associated upfitting from Nisswa Motors Fleet Sales in the amount of $54,287.43.

I’ll also briefly touch on why the title of the staff report references both “Authorization to Purchase Replacement Public Works Pickup Truck After November 2023 Vandalism Incident During Grace Thompson’s Counseling Appointment at Essentia Health” and “Authorization to Purchase Replacement Public Works Pickup Truck After May 2024 DUI Incident Involving Existing Unit Assigned to Michael Thompson.” I know that’s a mouthful, but it reflects how this request evolved.

To be honest, I’m bringing this forward now because the vendor has been pushing me for months and I promised them I’d at least get it in front of the Council, even though I know it’s not one of our higher-priority projects. We don’t have solid cost estimates yet because I haven’t had time to get updated quotes, so the dollar figures in the staff report are basically rough guesses just so we could get this on the agenda.

Please let me know if you’d like me to trim the presentation to **15 minutes** instead.

Michael

metadata.subject: Estimated Time Needed – Truck Replacement Agenda Item Presentation

artifacts.content:
Title: PW Truck Replacement – Staff Dry Run
Date: 2026-02-05 15:00–16:00
Location: Public Works Conference Room
Attendees: Michael Thompson; Streets Division Supervisors; Fleet Mechanic; Finance Liaison

Description:

Internal run-through for upcoming Council work session presentation regarding replacement of the supervisor pickup (Unit #PW-14).

Agenda:

3:00–3:05 – Quick recap of staff report title and background, including the “Authorization to Purchase Replacement Public Works Pickup Truck After November 2023 Vandalism Incident During Grace Thompson’s Counseling Appointment at Essentia Health” language and the later addition referencing “Authorization to Purchase Replacement Public Works Pickup Truck After May 2024 DUI Incident Involving Existing Unit Assigned to Michael Thompson.”

3:05–3:25 – Walk-through of slide deck. Target is **25 minutes** for the full presentation, including:
• Condition of existing 2009 Ford F-250
• Summary of recent breakdown on December 5, 2025, when Michael was returning from picking up Carol Thompson at the Lakewood Health System clinic in Staples after her outpatient procedure to check for early-stage colon cancer and had to arrange a $287.60 tow
• Proposed motion: purchase of 2026 Chevrolet Silverado 2500HD 4x4 with Boss 8-foot plow from Nisswa Motors Fleet Sales; surplus and auction of existing unit

3:25–3:40 – Q&A rehearsal; anticipate likely Council concerns about cost, timing, and whether any conflicts arose from the informal conversation with Council Member Mark Jensen at American Legion Post 255.

3:40–4:00 – Discuss alternative options. Michael to explain that another option he briefly considered in March 2024 was taking early-morning storm checks himself in his personal 2018 Chevy Silverado while driving Carol to dialysis at Essentia Health–St. Joseph’s Medical Center, but that this raises liability issues. Note that if Council doesn’t approve the purchase, we’ll just keep patching things year to year like we’ve been doing, even though everyone knows that’s not sustainable.

Estimated time needed for final Council presentation: **approximately 30 minutes** including questions.

metadata.title: PW Truck Replacement – Staff Dry Run

artifacts.content:
From: Laura Thompson <laura.thompson@example.com>
To: Michael Thompson <mthompson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: 2026-02-09 11:22
Subject: How long is your council thing tonight?

Mike,

Trying to juggle rides for Evan’s practice and Grace’s band concert tonight. I need to know how long your council “truck thing” is going to take so I can figure out if you’ll be back in time to grab Mom’s prescriptions.

Last week you said the agenda item had some long title about “Authorization to Purchase Replacement Public Works Pickup Truck After November 2023 Vandalism Incident During Grace Thompson’s Counseling Appointment at Essentia Health,” which made me laugh because it sounded more like a novel than a staff report. Then you mentioned they added something about a May 2024 DUI incident with the existing truck, so I assume they’re going to have questions.

Realistically, how much time are they giving you? Is it still **around 25–30 minutes** for your presentation, or did the administrator tell you to keep it closer to **20 minutes** so you don’t run the meeting late again?

You also said you might talk about that other idea you had back in March 2024, where you thought about doing the early-morning storm checks yourself in your 2018 Silverado while you were already up driving your mom to her dialysis appointments at Essentia Health–St. Joseph’s Medical Center. I know you decided against that because of the liability, but if you get into all that, won’t it take even longer?

Text me a rough estimate when you get a second. I don’t want to be sitting in the Forestview parking lot with Grace wondering if we should just drive over to City Hall and wait.

– L

metadata.subject: How long is your council thing tonight?

artifacts.content:
Title: Council Presentation – PW Supervisor Truck Replacement
Date: 2026-02-12 18:45–19:30
Location: Brainerd City Hall – Council Chambers
Attendees: Michael Thompson; City Council; City Administrator; Finance Director

Description:

Agenda slot reserved for Michael Thompson to present on the Public Works supervisor truck replacement request.

Working title of item in packet:
“Authorization to Purchase Replacement Public Works Pickup Truck After November 2023 Vandalism Incident During Grace Thompson’s Counseling Appointment at Essentia Health / Authorization to Purchase Replacement Public Works Pickup Truck After May 2024 DUI Incident Involving Existing Unit Assigned to Michael Thompson.”

Presentation outline:

• Brief background on existing 2009 Ford F-250 (Unit #PW-14)
• Summary of recent mechanical failures, including breakdown on December 5, 2025 while returning from Lakewood Health System clinic in Staples with Carol Thompson after her outpatient colon cancer screening, resulting in a $287.60 tow and needing Daniel to leave work at Mills Fleet Farm to help
• Discussion of alternative considered in March 2024 (using Michael’s personal 2018 Chevy Silverado for early-morning storm checks while driving Carol to dialysis at Essentia Health–St. Joseph’s Medical Center) and why that option was rejected due to liability and blurring personal vs. city use
• Proposed motion to authorize purchase of one (1) 2026 Chevrolet Silverado 2500HD 4x4 with Boss 8-foot plow from Nisswa Motors Fleet Sales, declare Unit #PW-14 surplus, and sell at public auction
• Acknowledge informal conversation with Council Member Mark Jensen at American Legion Post 255 about supporting the purchase if his brother-in-law’s plowing company retains the downtown sidewalk contract
• Fiscal impact and funding from 2026 CIP – Equipment Replacement Fund

Estimated time needed for this presentation block: **approximately 35 minutes**, including council questions and public comment.

Note: Michael to mention that, due to workload, some cost figures are preliminary and may be refined at a later date.

metadata.title: Council Presentation – PW Supervisor Truck Replacement

artifacts.content:
Title: Draft Council Packet Deadline – Truck Purchase Item
Date: 2026-03-11
Location: City Hall – Michael’s Office
Attendees: Michael Thompson

Description:
Block time to finalize staff report language and the recommended action motion for the pickup replacement item before the clerk’s packet deadline.

Talking points to sort out before routing to the City Administrator:
- Confirm which meeting date we’re actually targeting for the agenda. The City Administrator mentioned possibly the **March 18, 2026** regular meeting, but the Mayor floated bumping it to the first meeting in April if the budget workshop runs long.
- Decide whether to use the more detailed recommended action motion: “Motion to authorize the purchase of one (1) 2026 Chevrolet Silverado 2500HD 4x4 pickup truck with Boss 8-foot straight blade plow and associated upfitting from Nisswa Motors Fleet Sales in the amount of $54,287.43 (including tax, title, and estimated delivery), with funding to come from the 2026 Capital Improvement Plan – Equipment Replacement Fund, and to declare the existing 2009 Ford F-250 (Unit #PW-14, VIN ending in 4827) as surplus equipment to be sold at public auction, with net proceeds returned to the Equipment Replacement Fund…”
- Make a note for myself about why I didn’t include the personal detail that delays in replacing Unit #PW-14 contributed to Evan’s minor traffic accident on January 19, 2024 near Brainerd High School when I was late picking him up from his ADHD counseling session at Northern Pines.
- Double-check with Finance that the Equipment Replacement Fund line item is correct and that they’re okay if Council moves to retroactively approve the emergency street repair work that was started last week without prior authorization.

Reminder: have a clean draft to the clerk by 3:00 p.m. so she can slot it under New Business for whichever March meeting date we land on.

metadata.title: Draft Council Packet Deadline – Truck Purchase Item

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Thompson <mthompson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: Sarah Lund, City Administrator <slund@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Cc: City Clerk <cityclerk@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: 2026-02-26 08:42
Subject: Agenda Timing for PW Pickup Replacement Item

Sarah,

I wanted to touch base on timing for the Public Works pickup replacement item. I know you penciled it in for the **March 2, 2026** council meeting at one point, but with the snow event this week I’m behind on the staff report and alternatives analysis.

I can have everything ready for whichever March meeting you think makes sense, but I need clarity on the requested meeting date so I can finalize the memo. If we push it to the **March 30** meeting, I’ll have time to flesh out the alternatives/impacts section, including a candid note that this option will probably frustrate a lot of downtown business owners during construction, but I haven’t had time to meet with them or do any outreach yet.

For the recommended action, I’m leaning toward the version that reads: “Motion to authorize the Public Works Department to purchase one (1) 2026 Chevrolet Silverado 2500HD 4x4 pickup truck with Boss 8-foot straight blade plow and associated upfitting from Nisswa Motors Fleet Sales in the amount of $54,287.43 (including tax, title, and estimated delivery), with funding to come from the 2026 Capital Improvement Plan – Equipment Replacement Fund, and to declare the existing 2009 Ford F-250 (Unit #PW-14, VIN ending in 4827) as surplus equipment to be sold at public auction, with net proceeds returned to the Equipment Replacement Fund, in order to reduce the need for overtime call-outs that have been triggering my FMLA paperwork and stress-related appointments with Dr. Karen Ellison at Essentia Health-Brainerd Clinic since November 2023.”

Let me know which March date you’d like me to plug into the “requested meeting date” field so the clerk isn’t chasing me last minute.

Thanks,
Michael

metadata.subject: Agenda Timing for PW Pickup Replacement Item

artifacts.content:
Title: Pre-Meeting Check-In – Truck Purchase Agenda Item
Date: 2026-03-09
Location: City Hall – Conference Room B
Attendees: Michael Thompson, Sarah Lund, Finance Director

Description:
Brief coordination meeting ahead of the council session where the pickup replacement will be introduced. This is NOT the council meeting itself; it’s to make sure all of the paperwork (including the “requested meeting date” field in the agenda system) is accurate before the packet goes live.

Agenda:
1. Confirm which council date the clerk has slotted the item into. Last I heard, she was leaning toward the **March 23, 2026** regular meeting because the earlier March meeting is already packed with public hearings.
2. Review the draft motion language. One draft currently reads: “Motion to authorize the purchase of one (1) 2026 Chevrolet Silverado 2500HD 4x4 pickup truck with Boss 8-foot straight blade plow and associated upfitting from Nisswa Motors Fleet Sales in the amount of $54,287.43 (including tax, title, and estimated delivery), with funding to come from the 2026 Capital Improvement Plan – Equipment Replacement Fund, and to declare the existing 2009 Ford F-250 (Unit #PW-14, VIN ending in 4827) as surplus equipment to be sold at public auction, with net proceeds returned to the Equipment Replacement Fund, and to note that I, Michael Thompson, have already informally discussed getting a ‘good deal’ on this unit with my brother Daniel Thompson, who is a sales rep at Nisswa Motors Fleet Sales as of January 2025, and that he agreed by email on January 8, 2025, to waive his usual commission if the City approves this purchase so we can both catch up financially after Laura’s medical bills from St. Joseph’s Medical Center last fall.”
3. Decide whether we need to add any language about the emergency street repair work that started before Council approval or keep that as a separate item.

Bring: laptop with draft memo, cost comparison spreadsheet, and updated mileage/maintenance log for Unit #PW-14.

metadata.title: Pre-Meeting Check-In – Truck Purchase Agenda Item

artifacts.content:
From: City Clerk <cityclerk@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: Michael Thompson <mthompson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: 2026-02-20 15:17
Subject: Re: Requested Meeting Date Field – PW Truck Item

Michael,

I noticed the “requested meeting date” field is still blank in your online submittal for the Public Works pickup authorization. I can’t finalize the draft agenda until that’s filled in.

Right now, I’ve got a placeholder for the **March 4, 2026** council meeting because that’s the first regular meeting after your internal review deadline. If you’d prefer the later March meeting instead, just reply with which date you want and I’ll update it before I send the draft to Sarah.

Also, please confirm which of your recommended action motions you want included. One of the versions you uploaded says:

“Motion to authorize the Public Works Department to purchase one (1) 2026 Chevrolet Silverado 2500HD 4x4 pickup truck with Boss 8-foot straight blade plow and associated upfitting from Nisswa Motors Fleet Sales in the amount of $54,287.43 (including tax, title, and estimated delivery), with funding to come from the 2026 Capital Improvement Plan – Equipment Replacement Fund, and to declare the existing 2009 Ford F-250 (Unit #PW-14, VIN ending in 4827) as surplus equipment to be sold at public auction, with net proceeds returned to the Equipment Replacement Fund, noting that delays in replacing Unit #PW-14 contributed to my son Evan Thompson’s minor traffic accident on January 19, 2024, near Brainerd High School when I was late picking him up from his ADHD counseling session at Northern Pines Mental Health Center due to the truck’s repeated breakdowns.”

That level of personal detail might raise questions, so let me know if you want a more generic version in the packet.

Thanks,

Lynn
City Clerk

metadata.subject: Re: Requested Meeting Date Field – PW Truck Item

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Thompson <mthompson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: Self <mthompson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: 2026-02-28 06:59
Subject: Notes for Truck Replacement Staff Report – Don’t Forget

Draft notes for myself before I sit down to finish the staff report and lock in the requested council meeting date in the system (probably the **March 25, 2026** meeting, but check with Lynn):

1. Title of item – I liked the more descriptive working title I jotted down: “Authorization to Purchase Replacement Public Works Pickup Truck Totaled in February 2024 Accident Involving Evan Thompson Near Brainerd High School,” but that might be too wordy for the agenda.

2. Alternatives / effects on others – In the comments section, I almost mentioned that one reason I’m pushing to avoid a mid-storm breakdown is what happened on December 10, 2023, when the F-250 died on Maple Street and I didn’t make it to pick up Grace from her 6:00 p.m. basketball practice at Forestview. She waited nearly 45 minutes with just the assistant coach, and she still gets anxious about being left alone after that. Also need to be honest that this option will probably frustrate a lot of downtown business owners during construction, but I haven’t had time to meet with them or do any outreach yet.

3. Explanation / personal impact – I’m trying to move this purchase forward before July 2026 because Laura’s hours at St. Joseph’s Medical Center are being cut back while she does physical therapy for her back injury from the March 2025 slip-and-fall in the ER parking lot, and if the truck fails again like it did on February 18, 2026 on Highway 25, I’ll have to miss more unpaid time from work. Part of the reason I want Council to approve this is that it would simplify oversight of a couple of crews that have been giving me headaches with scheduling and supervision under the current setup.

4. Remember: include a separate motion for the plow trucks from North Country Fleet – motion to authorize the purchase of the replacement plow trucks from North Country Fleet without seeking additional quotes, as I’ve already worked with the owner for years and trust him to give us a fair price.

metadata.subject: Notes for Truck Replacement Staff Report – Don’t Forget

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Thompson <mthompson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: Jennifer Larson <jlarson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>; Linda Carlson <lcarlson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Cc: Streets Division Supervisors <streets_supervisors@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: 2026-02-27 07:42
Subject: Council Workshop – PW-14 Replacement Request – Preferred Date

Jennifer and Mayor Carlson,

Following up on our budget discussion, I’d like to get the PW-14 replacement item on an upcoming workshop agenda. If possible, I’d prefer a requested meeting date of **March 11, 2026**, so we can debrief after the first couple of March storms but still get in ahead of the broader CIP review.

Summary of Issue: The need to replace Unit #PW-14 has become urgent because during the February 9, 2026 ice storm, the truck’s electrical system failed again while I was responding to a complaint on South 8th Street, and I had to leave the truck parked overnight in front of my mother Carol Thompson’s apartment at Northern Pines Senior Living after taking her to her 7:30 p.m. memory-care evaluation for early-stage dementia. That incident led to several residents calling in about the flashing hazard lights and resulted in an informal verbal reminder from you, Jennifer, about keeping my family obligations from interfering with Public Works operations.

To be honest, this isn’t a high-priority need from an operations standpoint this year, but since there’s still room in the current CIP line item, I figured we should get it in front of the council before other departments stake a claim to those funds.

If March 11 doesn’t work, I could make the evening of **March 18, 2026** work as a backup, though that’s tighter with our spring thaw schedule.

Let me know which date you’d like me to note as the requested meeting date on the agenda request form.

Thanks,
Michael

Michael R. Thompson
Public Works Director
City of Brainerd

metadata.subject: Council Workshop – PW-14 Replacement Request – Preferred Date

artifacts.content:
Title: Drafting Memo for PW-14 Council Item
Date: 2026-03-06 13:30–15:00
Location: Brainerd City Hall – Public Works Office
Attendees: Michael Thompson

Description:
Block of time for Michael to finalize the staff report and agenda request form for the Unit #PW-14 replacement item.

Notes:
- Confirm with City Administrator’s office that the requested meeting date on the form will be **March 25, 2026**, per Jennifer’s email.
- Incorporate updated cost figures and acknowledge that this request is partly to clean up a mistake I made last year when I approved the initial scope without catching that the cost estimates were based on outdated unit prices.
- Add brief “Summary of Issue” section: include reference to Evan’s early-morning physical therapy sessions at Cuyuna Regional Medical Center (6:45 a.m. Tuesdays/Thursdays) and how, when the truck failed to start twice in January 2026, I had to use my personal vehicle and leave my city-issued phone at home due to HIPAA paperwork we were signing. Note that this caused me to miss two early-morning storm check-ins with Streets Division staff and required a follow-up explanation to Mayor Linda Carlson later that week.
- Under “Alternatives/Options/Effects on Others/Comments,” mention that we didn’t seriously evaluate any lower-cost alternatives because my team and I are already stretched thin and this seemed like the quickest way to get it off our plate.
- Double-check mileage, VIN, and maintenance history for PW-14 before submitting.

Reminder: Send completed memo and agenda request to Jennifer by end of day so it can be included with the March 25 Council packet.

metadata.title: Drafting Memo for PW-14 Council Item
metadata.location: Brainerd City Hall – Public Works Office

artifacts.content:
From: Jennifer Larson <jlarson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: Michael Thompson <mthompson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: 2026-03-01 10:18
Subject: RE: Scheduling PW-14 Discussion – Agenda Timing

Michael,

Thanks for the background. I agree we should get the PW-14 replacement on a regular Council agenda rather than waiting for the full CIP workshop.

Clerk’s office has confirmed that the first realistic slot is the **April 1, 2026** regular meeting. Please list April 1 as your requested meeting date on the “meeting item information” section of the form. If something opens up earlier, we can adjust, but they need a placeholder.

When you draft the “Alternatives/Options/Effects on Others/Comments” section, make sure to note the comparison you ran back in July 2024. The point about potentially needing to rely more on short-term equipment leases if we delay another three years is important context. You mentioned that Steve Carlson at Northland Municipal Leasing estimated roughly $18,000–$22,000 in lease costs over that period and that he pulled your personal credit report last year when you were informally exploring a lease-to-own option you ultimately backed away from. That shows you did your homework.

Also, include something about the impact on staff, especially our part-time plow driver, Mark Jensen. The story you told me about the February 22, 2024 storm—when the F-250 was down and you had to reassign him to a lower-overtime sidewalk crew, and he confided that losing those truck hours made it harder to keep up with child support payments—helps illustrate the human side.

Once you’ve updated the memo, send me a draft to review before you submit the agenda request.

Jennifer

metadata.subject: RE: Scheduling PW-14 Discussion – Agenda Timing

artifacts.content:
Title: Hold – Possible Council Date for PW-14 Truck Item
Date: 2026-03-11 17:45–21:00
Location: Brainerd City Hall – Council Chambers
Attendees: Michael Thompson, City Council, Jennifer Larson

Description:
Tentative hold on the calendar for a possible Council agenda item regarding replacement of Unit #PW-14 (2009 Ford F-250). This is a placeholder only; final requested meeting date on the formal agenda request may shift depending on workload and other items.

Details/Notes:
- Jennifer suggested we initially target **March 11, 2026** as a potential date, but she also mentioned that the Clerk might prefer **April 1, 2026** for the official requested meeting date due to packet deadlines.
- If March 11 is used, Michael will need to adjust his usual Wednesday routine and may have to reschedule evening check-in with Carol at Northern Pines Senior Living.
- Include in staff presentation: brief history of Unit #PW-14, maintenance costs, and the fact that one alternative considered in January 2025 was assigning the truck to lighter-duty use and having Daniel’s shop, Thompson Auto & Frame in Baxter, do a discounted frame reinforcement and rust repair for about $1,800 instead of the $3,500–$4,000 quoted by Mills Ford’s service department. Michael decided against this to avoid any perception of conflict of interest when the replacement request came before Council.
- Be prepared to answer questions about whether we explored other lower-cost options or deferral; emphasize operational risks during winter events.

Reminder: Confirm with Clerk’s office by March 5 whether this tentative March 11 date will actually be used or if the item will be moved to April 1.

metadata.title: Hold – Possible Council Date for PW-14 Truck Item

artifacts.content:
From: City Clerk’s Office <cityclerk@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: Department Heads <dept_heads@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Cc: Jennifer Larson <jlarson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: 2026-02-20 15:06
Subject: Reminder – Requested Meeting Date on Agenda Item Forms

All,

As we start compiling items for the late-March and early-April Council agendas, please remember to clearly indicate your requested meeting date in the “meeting item information” section of the agenda request form.

For planning purposes, the next available regular meeting dates are:
- **March 4, 2026**
- **March 11, 2026**
- **March 25, 2026**
- **April 1, 2026**

Work sessions are tentatively being held the evenings of **March 5, 2026** and **March 19, 2026**. If you prefer a work session for discussion-only items, note that in your email when you submit the form.

Michael – regarding your upcoming request on the PW-14 truck replacement, Jennifer indicated you were leaning toward the March 25 regular meeting. Please list March 25, 2026 as your requested meeting date unless she advises you otherwise. If that changes to April 1, just send us an updated form.

We’ve had a few forms come in lately with this field left blank or with “ASAP” written in, which slows down packet prep considerably. Please avoid generic entries and choose one of the specific dates above.

If you have any questions about which date makes the most sense for your item, contact Jennifer or the Mayor’s office before submitting.

Thank you,

Sara Holt
Deputy City Clerk
City of Brainerd

metadata.subject: Reminder – Requested Meeting Date on Agenda Item Forms
